title: Kesar Pista Recipe | #shorts
full_text: Hi, namaste. Auntie Zara here. Now, if you're from India, you're probably familiar with a flavor called Queser Pista. It's like saffron and pistachio and all these nuts mixed together. And it's a flavoring you get in milk, in ice cream, and a lot of other desserts, especially. I love that flavor, but I've never been able to find it here. And I love putting that flavor in my kids oatmeal and making it taste like dessert. So, I'm making my own homemade queser pista for Zoya to take to college. First, I put the raw almonds in the chopper. It's just a it's just a nut chopper. Then adding raw pistachios to the mix. Added the the saffron. Now, it's just saffron strands. See, look. And we're going to mix it. We're going to mix it nicely. Okay. And this whole mix can just stay in a box that then my daughter whenever she wants to eat oatmeal, she can scoop one scoop or two scoops of this out, mix and add it and top it to her oatmeal. Then you can add berries to it or whatever else sweetness you want. I don't like adding too much sweetness, but she does. Like she'll drizzle some uh you know what she'll do sometimes she'll drizzle some almond butter on it or peanut butter on it or whatever she wants. That's fine. That's the easy stuff. But I usually send her to college with a big whack of this and she loves adding it to her oatmeal. Thanks. Bye.
